branches.condition: Developer chooses to self-host instead of using Appwrite Cloud
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds initial installation and server startup steps before project creation

candidate_paths.name: Self-hosted Appwrite server setup
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Alternative initial setup before using the console and SDKs.
candidate_paths.selection_basis: Alternative to using Appwrite Cloud; involves installing and running Appwrite locally via Docker.

steps.action: Install Docker and run Appwrite Docker container
details: Run docker command to install Appwrite server
steps.failure_or_wait: Docker errors or server startup delays
Server not started or network issues

steps.action: Create account and project in console

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Project created with database and collection in Appwrite console

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer has a backend project with a database collection ready to be used by their app
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: New project and database collection appear in the Appwrite console UI
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Create a new project in the Appwrite console and configure a database with a collection
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This milestone represents the minimal backend setup enabling the developer to start integrating Appwrite services into their app, as shown in quickstart tutorials.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has Docker installed if self-hosting
entry_point.developer_intent: Create an Appwrite account and project to build a backend for a web or mobile application.

friction_gates.description: Creating an Appwrite account is required to access the cloud console and create projects.
Docker installation is required for self-hosting Appwrite locally.
SDK initialization requires correct endpoint URL and project ID configuration.
API calls require valid credentials or API keys depending on client type (client or admin).

prerequisites.requirement: Create an Appwrite account via the Appwrite Cloud or self-host an Appwrite server
Have Docker installed if self-hosting Appwrite locally

primary_path.action: Sign up for an Appwrite account or log in
details: Create an account or log in
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Error messages on invalid input or network issues

primary_path.action: Create a new project in the Appwrite console
details: Enter project name and select hosting region
primary_path.output: New project created and visible in console
primary_path.success_signal: Project dashboard loads with project details

primary_path.action: Create a new database within the project
primary_path.output: Database created and listed in project

primary_path.action: Create a collection (table) in the database
primary_path.output: Collection created and visible in database

details: Navigate to Attributes tab in collection

primary_path.action: Create a document in the collection
Add a platform for the app (web, mobile, native)
details: In project settings, click 'Add a platform'
primary_path.input: Platform type and identifier (domain, package ID)

primary_path.action: Initialize Appwrite SDK in app code with endpoint and project ID
details: Configure SDK with endpoint URL and project ID
primary_path.output: SDK client instance ready for API calls

primary_path.action: Make first API call to fetch or create data using SDK
details: Use SDK methods to interact with database or authentication
primary_path.output: API response with data or confirmation

alternatives_considered.name: Self-hosted CLI or direct API calls
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: While possible, the primary documented onboarding flow uses the web console and SDKs rather than CLI or raw API calls.

surface.name: Web Console and SDKs
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation and quickstarts emphasize the use of the Appwrite web console for project and backend setup, and SDKs for client and server integration.

uncertainties.impact: The boundary of first success is inferred from multiple steps rather than a single explicit milestone, which may affect precision.
uncertainties.question: Is there an officially documented single 'first success' milestone explicitly named by Appwrite?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: No explicit single milestone labeled as 'first success' was found in the official docs; the milestone was synthesized from quickstart and tutorial steps.

uncertainties.impact: Time estimate is approximate based on tutorial narrative and may vary.
uncertainties.question: Exact time to first success is not explicitly stated by Appwrite.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: No explicit timing claim found in official docs; estimate derived from tutorial video narration.
